scope_note: QA Norm Form and Split Prime Trichotomy cert [414]. Primary sources: Hecke, E. (1920) 'Eine neue Art von Zetafunktionen und ihre Beziehungen zur Verteilung der Primzahlen' doi:10.1007/BF01453601 (L-functions of quadratic fields, norm forms for Z[phi]); Cox, D. (1989) 'Primes of the form x^2+ny^2: Fermat, class field theory and complex multiplication' ISBN 978-0-471-19079-0 (Chapter 1: binary quadratic forms and prime splitting; Theorem 1.1: prime p is represented by norm form iff (disc/p)>=0). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The QA Eisenstein form f(a,b)=a^2+ab-b^2 derived in cert [133] from the QA T-step sign-flip identity f(e,b+e)=-f(b,e) is EQUAL to the algebraic norm form N_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(a+b*phi) where phi=(1+sqrt5)/2 (ring of integers Z[phi] of Q(sqrt5)). This equality is not circular: cert [133] derives f from QA discrete dynamics; the norm form is defined independently via field theory. The equality implies a three-way prime trichotomy: (C1) algebraic identity N(a+b*phi)=a^2+ab-b^2 via phi+phi_bar=1 and phi*phi_bar=-1 (both integers; Theorem NT: no sqrt5 or float in QA layer); (C2) SPLIT primes (p%5 in {1,4}): primitive integer solution f(a,b)=+/-p EXISTS, verified for 22 split primes <=193 by exhaustive search (falsifiable: any inert prime with a solution refutes); (C3) INERT primes (p%5 in {2,3}): NO primitive integer solution, verified for 22 inert primes <=193 (falsifiable: any solution found refutes); (C4) RAMIFIED p=5: generators (2,1) and (3,-1) both have norm 5, and their ratio (2+phi)/(3-phi)=(1+phi)=phi^2 is in Z[phi] (unit, norm=1), proving they generate the SAME ideal p_5; contrast with split p=11 where (3+phi)/(4-phi) is NOT in Z[phi] (numerator 10 not divisible by N(4,-1)=11), proving distinct ideals p and p_bar; all verified by exact integer arithmetic (no float, Theorem NT); (C5) THEOREM NT ZERO LOCUS: f(a,b)=0 over reals iff (2a+b)^2=5b^2 iff (2a+b)/b=+/-sqrt5 (irrational); no integer solution with (a,b)!=(0,0) verified for |a|,|b|<=30; the zero set of the norm form is the golden-ratio line b/a=(sqrt5-1)/2=1/phi (observer projection); the QA-certifiable region is the integer lattice f(a,b)=+/-p; Theorem NT boundary (continuous vs discrete) aligns exactly with the number-theoretic split/inert/ramified boundary. Bridge: cert [133] Eisenstein form (QA-native, derived from T-step) = cert [414] norm form (number-theory-native, derived from Z[phi]); the two arrived at the same algebraic object from independent directions. Chain: [133]->[414]->[410]->[413]. Derived 2026-06-14.
